name: Papel Cliffs and Punta Arxente - circular from Porto de Morás via the Natural Path of the Cantabrian Route
name_original: Acantilados de Papel y Punta Arxente – circular desde Porto de Morás por el Camino Natural de la Ruta del Cantabrico
region_intro: Hiking around Xove offers exploration of a rugged coastline, unique geological formations, and serene natural paths. The region is characterized by dramatic granite cliffs, sculpted by the Cantabrian Sea, and features numerous coves and beaches. Inland, green landscapes with riverbanks and woodlands provide diverse terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Xove The most popular hiking route is Papel Cliffs and Punta Arxente - circular from Porto de Morás via the Natural Path of the Cantabrian Route, an easy 3.7 miles (6.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This route showcases the distinctive folded granite formations of the Cliffs of Paper. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Illa de Sarón – Cabo de Morás loop from ZEPA Costa da Mariña Occidental, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views of the coastline and offshore islets. Local hikers also love the Roncadoiro Lighthouse – Ruins and Excavations loop from O Neto Grande, a 3.6 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes with views of the Roncadoira Lighthouse, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Hiking around Xove is defined by dramatic coastal cliffs, picturesque beaches, and inland riverbanks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy coastal walks to moderate loops. The routes in Xove are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Xove's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Xove?
A: Xove offers a diverse network of over 10 hiking routes, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These include easy coastal walks, moderate loops, and some more challenging paths, all providing unique ways to explore the region's natural beauty.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Xove?
A: The hiking trails in Xove are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 1,400 hikers have explored the area, often praising the dramatic coastal scenery, unique geological formations, and well-maintained paths.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Xove?
A: Xove's coastal climate means hiking is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, ideal for exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4617323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Paper&lt;/a&gt; and coastal paths. Summer is popular for combining hikes with beach visits, while winter can offer dramatic, rugged coastal views, though some paths might be more exposed to the elements.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Xove?
A: Yes, Xove has several easy trails perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed walk. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975158368/papel-cliffs-and-punta-arxente-circular-from-porto-de-moras-via-the-natural-path-of-the-cantabrian-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Papel Cliffs and Punta Arxente - circular from Porto de Morás&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6 km route showcasing the distinctive folded granite formations. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31919292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roncadoiro Lighthouse – Ruins and Excavations loop from O Neto Grande&lt;/a&gt;, offering coastal views.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Xove?
A: While many trails in Xove are moderate, some routes offer more elevation gain and distance for a challenging experience. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31912529" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Faro loop from Shower&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.8 km hike with significant ascents and descents, providing a good workout amidst stunning coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Xove?
A: Yes, Xove features several rewarding circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10606845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Illa de Sarón – Cabo de Morás loop from ZEPA Costa da Mariña Occidental&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.2 km path offering panoramic views of the coastline and offshore islets. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975158368/papel-cliffs-and-punta-arxente-circular-from-porto-de-moras-via-the-natural-path-of-the-cantabrian-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Papel Cliffs and Punta Arxente circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 6 km loop.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Xove?
A: Yes, for those seeking the tranquility of waterfalls, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31909842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo da Ferida Waterfall loop from O Cal&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.2 km hike. This route takes you through lush inland greenery to discover the beautiful Pozo da Ferida Waterfall, offering a refreshing change from the coastal paths.

Q: Which trails offer the best viewpoints in Xove?
A: Xove is renowned for its spectacular viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10606845" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Illa de Sarón – Cabo de Morás loop&lt;/a&gt; provides extensive coastal panoramas. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31919292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roncadoiro Lighthouse loop&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning vistas from Punta Roncadoira, including views of various islets. Mount Castelo also provides a commanding perspective of Xove's beautiful terrain.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Xove suitable for families with children?
A: Many of Xove's trails are suitable for families, especially the easier coastal walks and inland river paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975158368/papel-cliffs-and-punta-arxente-circular-from-porto-de-moras-via-the-natural-path-of-the-cantabrian-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Papel Cliffs and Punta Arxente circular route&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option that offers unique geological formations to capture children's imaginations. The Ruta de los Molinos, following the Rigueira River, is another low-difficulty, engaging option with historic mills.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Xove?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of Xove's hiking trails, especially the natural paths and coastal routes. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, particularly in areas with wildlife or near cliffs, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas where restrictions might apply. Always remember to clean up after your pet.

Q: What are the parking options for hiking trails in Xove?
A: Parking is generally available at key access points for popular trails in Xove. For coastal routes like the Sendero Costero de Xove, you can often find parking near beaches such as O Portiño de Morás or Esteiro Beach. For specific trailheads, it's recommended to check the route details on komoot for designated parking areas or nearby village parking.

Q: Is it possible to reach the hiking trails in Xove by public transport?
A: While Xove is a smaller municipality, some public transport options, primarily bus services, connect it to larger towns in the Mariña Lucense region. However, direct public transport access to specific trailheads might be limited. Planning your route in advance and checking local bus schedules is recommended if you intend to rely on public transport for trail access.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Xove?
A: Xove's hiking trails offer access to stunning natural attractions. You can witness the extraordinary &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4617323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of Paper&lt;/a&gt;, with their unique folded granite formations, or enjoy panoramic views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point&lt;/a&gt;. The Roncadoira Point, with its iconic lighthouse, also provides spectacular coastal vistas and opportunities for birdwatching. Inland, you can explore green landscapes and riverbanks.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore near Xove's trails?
A: Yes, beyond its natural beauty, Xove offers some interesting historical and cultural landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gate of Charles V&lt;/a&gt;, a historical site, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3171884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Atalaia Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, a man-made monument offering great views. The Ruta de los Molinos also showcases nine historic horizontal-wheel mills, providing a glimpse into Galician rural heritage.
tips: Beautiful cliffs located near the point of Morás. With a height of about 40 meters, these granite rock formations, sculpted by the wind and the waves of the sea, owe their name to their appearance in the form of folds reminiscent of sheets of paper.

On the Xove coast, following the path, is Cabo de Morás, a natural wonder sculpted by the force of the Cantabrian Sea hitting the coast.
The loud sound of the water hitting the rocks, together with the spectacular views of the Farallóns islets to the east and As Salseiras and the Ansarón islet to the west, invite you to admire its beauty.
Excellent viewpoint of the Cantabrian Sea located on the Xove coast with views of the Ansarón islet, As Salseiras and the Papel cliffs.
A perfect point from which to contemplate the island of Ansarón, one of the largest on the Lugo coast with some 10 hectares of extension.

waypoints.name: View of Ansarón Islet and As Salseiras Cliffs from Atalaya Gepeto
